title: Implement ZIP-32 master key derivation (unblock nie-m91)
description: Implement BIP-39 mnemonic → 64-byte seed → ZIP-32 Sapling master key using the workaround chosen by the spike bead. Deliver: derive_master_key(seed: &[u8; 64]) -> SaplingMasterKey, derive_account_key(master: &SaplingMasterKey, account: u32) -> SaplingExtendedSpendingKey. Defensive programming: (1) assert_ne\!(identity_seed, wallet_seed) at wallet init — key separation invariant, panic with 'wallet and identity keys must be independent'; (2) SaplingMasterKey must NOT implement Debug or Clone; (3) validate seed length == 64 with descriptive Err not panic; (4) zeroize key material on drop using zeroize crate. Use a subagent to look up the ZIP-32 spec PRF^expand domain separation byte (0x01 for child key) and hardcode it as a named constant with a doc comment citing the spec section.
design: Never derive wallet key from identity key or vice versa. Never implement Debug on key material structs.
acceptance_criteria: cargo test --workspace passes. ZIP-32 derive round-trip test using hardcoded known test vector from zcash spec (not derived from code under test). Key separation assert present and tested.
close_reason: Already implemented in nie-core/src/wallet.rs using blake2b_simd (option b from spike). WalletMasterKey::from_seed() uses BLAKE2b-512 with ZcashIP32Sapling personalization. All acceptance criteria met: test vector from Python oracle, key separation assert in CLI init path, all tests passing. Child key derivation (derive_account_key) requires jubjub crate — tracked by nie-1ws/nie-fz7.
